title: veronica ciliata subsp. cephaloides
intro: Veronica ciliata subsp. cephaloides, also known as the hairy speedwell or Alpine Veronica, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Veronica ciliata subsp. cephaloides include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds, while also exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, with some herbal preparations also recommending the use of the plant's leaves and flowers in salves and creams for topical application.
